pattern: Use two pointers from both ends and, on the first mismatch, allow exactly one deletion by testing whether either inner substring is already a palindrome.
hints: Compare characters from the outside in; as long as they match keep moving both pointers toward the center.
When you hit the first mismatch you must spend your single allowed deletion on one of the two offending characters.
Check whether skipping the left character or skipping the right character leaves a palindrome; if either does, the answer is true, otherwise it is false.

edge_inputs.description: one deletion makes it a palindrome
single character is trivially a palindrome
mismatch resolvable only by removing the left side

stress.description: random short lowercase strings exercising both outcomes
stress.note: small alphabet raises the chance of near-palindromes

stress.description: genuine palindromes with a single character corrupted to test the one-deletion path
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    half = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    left = [rng.choice('abcd') for _ in range(half)]
    s = left + left[::-1]
    if rng.random() < 0.7 and s:
        i = rng.randrange(len(s))
        s[i] = rng.choice('abcd')
    return [''.join(s)]
